summary: I compiled the iteration-2 review report at `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/review-report-writer/output/review_report.md`. To ground it in current reality I read both review critics' verdicts (requirements-conformance: **pass** on all 50 requirements R1–R50; code-quality: **needs_work** driven purely by performance/consistency, with no correctness/security defects) and the Build test-critic's iteration-2 verdict (**pass** — 213 tests/738 assertions green, rubocop clean, system tests blocked only by a local chromedriver/Chrome mismatch). I then inspected the iteration-2 implementer diff and confirmed it **resolved all four code-quality findings** (broadcast_card `dashboard:` flag skipping progress-tick fan-out, per-source DB `LIMIT`s in recent_activity, single manager-tick broadcast, and symmetric stuck/unstuck sweep broadcasts), while verifying via git history and file contents that the **five UI style-guide findings (F1–F5) from my input's feedback remain open** in the current build (palette `-500` vs `-600` drift, hand-rolled attention pill vs `status_badge`, business logic in `DefineHelper` rather than a PORO, three action-less empty states, and the deliberate fleet-polling deviation). The rewritten PR-ready report separates these into a "Resolved findings" table (the four fixed code-quality items) and an "Open findings" table (the five minor UI conformance items, with UI-5 flagged for a manager decision), and recommends merge with the minor cleanups and CI system-test check as fast-follows.
